title: CommentatorsNotebook
description: A deterministic, source-backed set of match facts computed by a fixed, pre-registered family of templates over the vendored CC0 packs. Every fact is labelled predictive / context / coincidence, carries its sample, denominator, base rate, source ids and freshness, and is sample-guarded. Coincidences are capped and quarantined. This is a read model derived from the match history; it NEVER carries or alters a forecast probability, calibration metric, or any engine number — see docs/handoff/codex-phase7.md for the machine-checked no-write invariant.

as_of_utc.description: The deterministic information horizon. Facts use only completed matches with a kickoff at or before this instant, so the notebook never sees data the sealed forecast could not.

source_ids.description: The vendored snapshot ids every fact is cited against.

family_size.description: The number of pre-registered fact hypotheses this fixed template family evaluates for one match. This is the multiple-comparison exposure bound: it is a constant of the release, not a function of the data. Adding a template raises it and is a logged, reviewed change.

coincidence_cap.description: The maximum number of coincidence-labelled facts the notebook will ever emit for one match.

suppressed.description: An audit trail of candidate facts a guardrail removed, so the guard is visible rather than silent.

Fact.description: One computed statement. `text` is number-disciplined: every digit that appears in it is one of `numbers` (so the fact can be folded into the AI evidence whitelist verbatim). Dates live only in `date_range`, never in `text`.

id.description: The registered template id this fact came from.

values.description: Structured numeric/string values behind the text, for machine consumers and the UI.

numbers.description: Every number that appears verbatim in `text`, with the exact display string. Drives the AI numeric whitelist when the fact is folded into an evidence bundle.

sample_n.description: The number of observations the statement is built on (streak length, meetings, matches in the base-rate window).

denominator.description: The base-rate denominator; equals sample_n for non-rate facts. A rate fact is suppressed unless denominator >= min_sample.

base_rate.description: The base rate as a fraction in [0,1] for rate facts, else null.

date_range.description: The [first, last] calendar date of the matches behind the fact.

min_sample.description: The template's minimum-sample threshold that this fact passed.

specificity.description: A deterministic rarity score used ONLY to rank facts (especially to cap coincidences). It is not a p-value and carries no claim of statistical significance.

staleness_days.description: The staleness window applied; null means the template does not go stale (structural facts).
